Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules.

context: SOURCE 3: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue Ticket number: TI-1623488 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 20 Owner: Employee 27 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 02.12.25 09:03 Closure Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21 Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again. Activities 02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents. All other employees in the same room can print without issue. The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print. 02.12.25 09:03 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27. 02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with the affected user. Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible. When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately. Print job is not displayed in the queue. 02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user Test performed with colleague in the same office. Printing works without restriction. User account checked. Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed. 02.12.25 10:30 – Further review Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. It specifies: • Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups • No individual user permissions at queue level • NTFS and share permissions must be consistent Review of the printer queue on the print server shows: • AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission • Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny” This entry originates from a previous test configuration. 02.12.25 11:10 – Test Explicit deny permission removed. User logged in again. Test print successful. 03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback Printing works normally again. Additional tests within the team successful. 03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 20 The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user. The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. Printing is now fully functional again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

Restart Print Spooler (fast, safe): - Elevated CMD: net stop spooler && net start spooler - Or PowerShell (Admin): Stop-Service -Name Spooler -Force; Start-Service -Name Spooler - After restart: check Settings → Printers & scanners. Try manual UNC add and quick network checks: - From the user’s session try: \\printserver in Explorer and then Add a printer → “The printer that I want isn’t listed” → Select a shared printer by name → \\printserver\printer (use the canonical printserver name you have). - Also run: ping printserver and net view \\printserver - Interpretation: if manual UNC add succeeds, GPO mapping was the issue; if UNC add fails with “Access denied” it suggests a queue ACL/deny entry; if it fails with “path not found” or ping fails while colleagues can reach it, suspect network/VLAN

Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules. SOURCE 3: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue Ticket number: TI-1623488 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 20 Owner: Employee 27 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 02.12.25 09:03 Closure Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21 Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again. Activities 02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents. All other employees in the same room can print without issue. The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print. 02.12.25 09:03 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27. 02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with the affected user. Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible. When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately. Print job is not displayed in the queue. 02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user Test performed with colleague in the same office. Printing works without restriction. User account checked. Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed. 02.12.25 10:30 – Further review Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. It specifies: • Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups • No individual user permissions at queue level • NTFS and share permissions must be consistent Review of the printer queue on the print server shows: • AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission • Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny” This entry originates from a previous test configuration. 02.12.25 11:10 – Test Explicit deny permission removed. User logged in again. Test print successful. 03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback Printing works normally again. Additional tests within the team successful. 03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 20 The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user. The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. Printing is now fully functional again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

context: SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Mouse lag while docked is unlikely related; treat it separately unless you see correlated spooler/driver errors. One‑line message you can paste to the helpdesk technician / user - "Run (as the user): net stop spooler && net start spooler; then gpupdate /force, sign out and sign back in. If printers are still empty, run gpresult /h \"%temp%\\gpresult.html\" and whoami /groups and attach those files/screenshots to the ticket. Also try opening \\printserver and attempting to add \\printserver\\<printer> — capture any error text. Escalate if gpresult shows GPO applied but UNC add returns Access denied or if spooler crashes." If you paste the gpresult.html (or the gpresult /r text) and the net view / ping outputs here I will read them and tell you the exact next remediation or which 2nd‑line team and change to request. References - GPO not applied / gpupdate resolved new‑user missing printers

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?

context: SOURCE 41: ﻿general info & description Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company Version: 1.0 Date: 15.10.2025 Author: IT Infrastructure Status: Approved This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company. The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles: • Central printer management via print servers • Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO) • Standardized driver versions • Location-based printer concepts • Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail The print infrastructure is based on: • Windows print servers • Location-based printer queues • User- or group-based assignment • VLAN-separated printer networks Manual installation of local printers is not intended. design Architecture overview User device → Print server → Printer (in printer VLAN) Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server. Printer provisioning Printers are: • Created as a queue on the print server • Configured with a standardized driver • Assigned via GPO based on location or department Default printers are set automatically. Driver standard Only approved and tested driver versions are used. After Windows updates, it must be verified: • Whether drivers remain compatible • Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active • Whether printers are still mapped correctly Scan-to-Mail Scan-to-Mail is performed: • Via a dedicated SMTP relay • Using a configured service account • Secured with TLS Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality. Location concept Each location has: • At least one default printer • One multifunction printer with scan capability • Assignment via AD groups Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN. decision tree & use cases Decision Tree Initial question: Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning? A) Printing affected A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”? • No → Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group. If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied. If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update. If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server. • Yes → Proceed to A2. A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue? • No → Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment). • Yes → Proceed to A3. A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue? • Yes → Check whether the queue is paused. If not → Check the Print Spooler service. If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN). • No → Proceed to A4. A4: Does the issue affect only one location? • Yes → Check VLAN reachability. Check DNS resolution of the printer. Check location-based GPO assignment. • No → Check driver compatibility or print server service. A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update? • Yes → Check driver compatibility. Check Point-and-Print policy. Validate security policies. • No → Further analysis according to standard process. B) Scanning affected B1: Is printing still working? • Yes → Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path. • No → First analyze printing issue according to section A. B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning? • SMTP or connection error → Check SMTP relay reachability. Check service account authentication credentials. Validate TLS configuration. • No error message, but no delivery → Check mail queue. Check spam/filter rules. SOURCE 22: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs Ticket number: TI-1622483 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 15 Owner: Employee 21 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 06.11.25 08:37 Closure Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52 Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored. Activities 06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15 Good morning Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed. Other printers in the building are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 06.11.25 08:37 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21. 06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis Remote access to an affected user. Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing. Queue itself is not paused. Print Spooler service is running. Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails. 06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location? Thank you for your brief feedback. 06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN. No other changes are known to us. 07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful. Ping from a client within the same location network successful. This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN. Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly. 07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN. Rule adjusted according to standard. 10.11.25 – Test phase Printing from print server successful. Multiple test prints from user workstations performed. No abnormalities observed. 12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback Printer is functioning normally again. Larger print jobs are also processed correctly. 12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 15 The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location. The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company
